key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 continuing OCF $6.49B − capex $0.97B ÷ 1.64B diluted shares (10-K)
MI300 ramp continues; Client stable; margin modest expansion
[Assumption] FY2027 normalized if Data Center sustains 35%+ revenue growth and margin expands

open_questions: [ ] Sign off on **10%** consolidated years 1–5 growth versus Data Center **16%** segment path
[ ] Review `ai_overlay.not_in_model_requires_refresh` (custom ASIC, foundry prepayments, MI350 margin)
thesis: Advanced Micro Devices designs high-performance CPUs, GPUs, and adaptive computing chips for data centers, client PCs, gaming consoles, and embedded markets. **[Fact]** Customers include hyperscale cloud providers, PC OEMs, console makers, and industrial OEMs. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$34.6 billion** (+34% versus last year); operating profit **$3.7 billion**; net income **$4.3 billion**. **[Fact]** Reportable segments are Data Center (**$16.6 billion**), Client and Gaming (**$14.6 billion** combined), and Embedded (**$3.5 billion**). **[Fact]** Cash to shareholders comes from continuing operating cash flow after capital spending and foundry prepayments.
why_market_wrong: There is **no clear dated mispricing signal** (HK predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** The market prices AMD as the credible number-two AI accelerator and server CPU challenger, embedding share-gain optionality against NVIDIA. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math sums to **~$96 per share** versus a **~$466** market price, so the debate is whether MI300 ramp margins and Data Center growth durability justify the premium when Lawrence base cash-flow math delivers only mid-single-digit returns unless bull-case share gains materialize.
